layout.description: Administrative tools affect the shared catalog and system-wide configuration. Treat this area as operational space, not day-to-day reading flow.
layout.scope_description: Users, import roots, and provider settings live here. Reader-specific activity stays in the main app.

confirmation.self_demote: Are you sure you want to remove your own admin privileges? You will be logged out of the admin dashboard.
confirmation.delete: Are you sure you want to delete {{user}}? This action cannot be undone.
confirmation.self_demote_message: You are about to remove your own admin privileges. The app will refresh and you will lose access to the admin control surface.
confirmation.delete_message: This will permanently remove {{user}} from the system. Their access and user-scoped overlay data may no longer be recoverable.

stats.total_description: Signed-in accounts with access to the shared catalog.
stats.admins_description: Users who can mutate system-wide settings and administrative flows.
stats.readers_description: Standard users who can access the shared catalog and maintain their own overlay data.

settings.title: System Settings
settings.subtitle: Configure global application settings and integrations.

cards.provider_model_description: Metadata integrations are currently driven by backend environment and deployment config rather than in-app forms.
cards.change_cadence_description: These controls should stay sparse and explicit because they affect catalog-wide ingestion and lookup behavior.
cards.current_state_description: The UI can describe active providers today while leaving mutation to deployment config until a stronger editing flow exists.

providers.title: Metadata Providers
providers.description: Metadata API keys (like Hardcover or Google Books) are currently read from environment variables.

library.title: Library Management
library.configure: Configure storage locations and discover new media.

stats.scope_description: Scanning can only target server directories explicitly allowlisted in backend configuration.
stats.impact_description: Scanned files do not publish directly. They enter staging for review, merge, and approval.
stats.best_use_description: Use direct upload for one-offs and this tool when the server already has a mounted collection ready to scan.

library.scan_root_hint: Configured import roots only (server env: IMPORT_SCAN_ROOTS)
library.path_hint: The backend will reject paths outside the configured import roots.
library.storage_paths_description: Library paths are currently managed via environment variables (STORAGE_PATH). Future updates will allow multiple library volumes and in-app configuration.
library.scan_description:
Trigger a manual filesystem scan to discover new book files (EPUB, MP3, etc.) placed directly into
                    your storage directories. Found items will be placed in the Staging Area.
library.prompt: Enter full absolute path to scan for books (or leave empty to scan the default library directory):
library.initialized: Library scan initialized. Books will appear in the Staging Area once discovered.
